item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: Online music store Bandcamp bans AI music
item.description: "﻿With this policy, we're putting human creativity first", the store said in a statement this week.

item.title: Cult Australian designer clothing brand Sass & Bide to close down within weeks
item.description: The Myer-owned label said the closure was not goodbye, but a "see you soon".

item.title: Iconic Melbourne retailer Fletcher Jones shutting down
item.description: The announcement is the latest blow to the nation's struggling retail sector.

item.title: Australian billionaires' wealth rose by $600,000 per day in 2025: new report
item.description: A new report has found the average Australian billionaire saw their wealth rise by almost $600,000 per day in 2025, while one in three households faced food insecurity.

item.title: Ampol's $1.1 billion takeover of servo network hits hurdle
item.description: The competition watchdog has failed to approve the deal until further investigation is conducted.

item.title: What's open on the Australia Day public holiday
item.description: Australia Day is a national public holiday but retail workers around the country will still be hard at work.

item.title: Australian dollar cracks 70 US cents on climb to three-year high
item.description: T﻿he local currency today was worth US70 cents, its highest since February 2023.

item.title: Australia's most valuable company continues climb after claiming crown from Commonwealth Bank
item.description: Mining giant BHP has consolidated its new crown as Australia's most valuable company, a day after knocking off the country's biggest bank at the top of the stock exchange.

item.title: Gold price soars to $US5300, as questions surround whether Aussie dollar rally will continue
item.description: E﻿xpects are not ruling out the precious metal hitting the $US6000 mark later this year.

item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: The three things that could save Aussies from looming interest rate hell
item.description: There's some hope for mortgage-holders, one of the big banks has said.

item.title: Tesla's sales slowdown puts Elon Musk's enormous pay deal at risk
item.description: Tesla has seen its first decline in annual revenue in the company's history, putting CEO Elon Musk's historic pay package deal at risk.

item.title: 'Cold shower' for mortgage holders: Experts' gloomy rates' forecast
item.description: E﻿conomists are backing the RBA to raise the cash rate next week, and any future relief could be months away.

item.title: Nine sells talkback radio assets to Laundy family for $56 million
item.description: An Aussie pub tycoon is making a venture into the world of the media with a big purchase.

item.title: Dragging Melbourne roadworks threaten to force locals out of business
item.description: Rebekah Becsi fears she'll have to close down her discount food store in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Gold price tumbles after Trump names pick to lead US Federal Reserve
item.description: I﻿nvestors are spurning gold and other precious metals in a vote of confidence for the man expected to lead the world's most powerful central bank.

item.title: Treasurer defends government spending ahead of expected rates hike
item.description: Mortgage holders are bracing for a rates hike today, but Jim Chalmers insists the government isn't to blame.

item.title: Iconic Sydney brewery to shut its doors
item.description: A popular brewery is set to shut its doors ﻿in Sydney's inner west in search of a tree change.

item.title: 'Silent strain': More than a million homeowners struggle with mortgage repayments
item.description: The latest cash rate rise is another hammer blow for struggling mortgage holders, new research has found.

item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Rio Tinto scraps $300 billion merger plan with rival Glencore
item.description: B﻿oth companies said any potential deal would fail to deliver shareholders enough value.

item.title: Australian luxury retailer Cosette set to close down after more than 11 years in business
item.description: An Australian luxury retailer is set to close down after more than 11 years in business.

item.title: Deal reached to save collapsed Healthscope private hospitals
item.description: H﻿ealthscope will become a not-for-profit operator in order to save its suite of hospitals after the company collapsed financially last year.

item.title: Australia quietly moves to impose tariffs on Chinese steel imports
item.description: T﻿he move follows concerns by local businesses they are facing unfair competition, but risks retaliation from Beijing.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: ANZ posts nearly $2 billion in quarterly profit, days after charging staff for 'welcome back' sausage sizzle
item.description: ANZ﻿'s cash profit for the first quarter jumped by six per cent to $1.94 billion, partly driven by the thousands of redundancies announced last year.

item.title: Iconic Australian retailer Barbeques Galore collapses into receivership﻿, 500 jobs at risk
item.description: Around 500 jobs are now at risk.

item.title: Australia Post wants to raise stamp prices
item.description: T﻿he national postal service says it's needed to offset losses in its letter division.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: NSW to get an extra public holiday for the next two years after Anzac Day, Premier confirms
item.description: T﻿he decision hasn't been universally popular, but Minns insists the decision is the right thing to do.

item.title: Higher GST, lower income tax, part of plan to lift Australia's economy
item.description: Strong economic medicine is needed over d﻿ebt levels racked up by Australian states, a global body warns.

item.title: Australian food court chain Sushi Sushi sells to Japanese dining empire in estimated $160 million deal
item.description: Sushi Sushi, which began in Melbourne in 1998, has been snapped ﻿up by Tokyo-based Genki Global Dining Concepts.

item.title: Australian gas producer Santos wins court fight over net zero claims
item.description: A lawsuit against gas producer Santos alleged the company misled the public on its plans to achieve net zero carbon emissions.

item.title: Record number of cafes and restaurants going out of business
item.description: T﻿he hospitality industry in Australia is now split between winners and losers, new research shows.

item.title: 'This is peak Australia': NAB chief warns living standards will stagnate without serious reform
item.description: After generations of improvement, our living standards will be stuck in the present – or go backwards – without major economic reform.

item.title: Business busted for making false claims about link to Bondi terror attack
item.description: N﻿SW Fair Trading found the company falsely claimed the proceeds of sales would go to victims and their families.

item.title: Telstra records billion dollar profit, reveals it shed 2356 jobs in 2025
item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.title: eBay to buy Depop in $1.7b move to reach Gen Z and Millennials
item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.title: Former South Korean president given life sentence for imposing martial law
item.description: A special prosecutor had demanded the death penalty for Yoon, saying his actions posed a threat to the country’s democracy.

item.title: Texts from China's alleged 'spy thriller' ring revealed
item.description: An alleged plot by Chinese citizens to infiltrate a religious network in Australia had "a bit of a spy thriller feel to it", according to court documents.

item.description: H﻿alf of those set to benefit are pensioners.

item.title: How a blood test could predict if and when you could get Alzheimer's
item.description: A﻿lzheimer's is the biggest cause of dementia, which is the leading cause of death in Australia.

item.description: H﻿e also accused former President Obama of leaking classified information.

item.description: But Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has made his stance abundantly clear.

item.title: Trump's made tariffs central to his presidency. Chaos may come next
item.description: Trump had made tariffs the bedrock of his economic pitch to voters ahead of the midterm elections, even describing tariffs as his "favourite word in the dictionary".

item.title: US President Donald Trump says he'll raise tariffs to 15 percent after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he wants a global tariff of 15 per cent.

item.title: The US shut down its Global Entry system amid government chaos. Here's what that means for Aussie travellers
item.description: The US just slammed its Global Entry system shut amid ongoing government chaos.

item.title: 'It's a hard no from me': Premier quashes call for daylight saving in south-east Queensland before Olympics
item.description: ﻿The discussion comes amid efforts to boost Brisbane's night-life economy ahead of the Brisbane 2032 OIympic Games.

item.title: Unions say controversial capital gains tax discount 'hurts workers'
item.description: Australian Unions say they are calling for an overhaul of capital gains tax concessions on property to "help drive a fairer housing system."

item.title: Gunman shot dead at Trump's Florida home of Mar-a-Lago was 'fixated on the Epstein files': report
item.description: An armed man who was shot dead at the US President Donald Trump's residence at Mar-a-Lago in Florida was fixated on the Epstein files, according to TMZ.

item.title: China to be 'on parity' with the United States in nuclear weapons within years, official warns
item.description: "[China] has deliberately, and without constraint, massively expanded its nuclear arsenal."

item.title: Employees at Aussie start-up Eucalyptus set to receive windfall after $1.6 billion acquisition
item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: High-speed rail hoped to ease housing crisis as government throws $230 million more at plan
item.description: T﻿he federal government has announced an additional $230 million to plan for a ﻿high-speed rail link between Sydney and Newcastle.

item.title: UK politicians approve release of confidential documents related to former Prince Andrew
item.description: Indignant UK politicians have lined up to criticise the former prince, with some calling to scrap a long-standing protection for the royal family.

item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.title: Anthony Albanese 'all good' after being evacuated from official residence in Canberra over security scare
item.description: A﻿nthony Albanese had to be evacuated from his official residence last night.

item.title: Domino's Pizza sheds 10 per cent of customers after reducing discounts
item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.title: Man fired after pouring cooking oil down the drain outside popular bakery
item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.description: T﻿he meeting at Trump's Florida residence happened just before a $1.5 billion announcement.

item.title: Australian jailed in US for seven years for selling stolen trade secrets to buyer with ties to Russia
item.description: The 39-year-old was accused of putting the United States and Australia at risk thanks to his crimes.

item.title: 'Cannot terminate at Sydney': PM's plan to extend high-speed rail project to Melbourne
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced he wants to extend a touted east-coast high-speed rail projec﻿t to Melbourne.

item.title: Trio of US politicians pushing for much higher tariffs on lamb from Australia and New Zealand
item.description: As Australia's sheep farmers began grappling with the impact of President Donald Trump's new round of tariffs, some US politicians were already pushing to make their lives even harder.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: PM Anthony Albanese apologises to Grace Tame after using the word 'difficult' to describe her in rapid-fire in
item.description: Former Australian of the Year and child abuse survivor Grace Tame has refused Anthony Albanese's apology after he used the word "difficult" to describe her during an interview.

item.title: Opposition calls for universities to scrap group assignments after fierce student backlash
item.description: "There is always that student who does the work, and that student who reaps the benefit."

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese defiant as Australian children detained in Syria plead to come home
item.description: Anthony Albanese has voiced compassion for the 23 Australian children detained in Syria, but remained defiant against their desperate pleas to return home.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.description: In a statement on Friday morning, Ley announced she had tendered her resignation, effective immediately﻿.﻿

item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: Government won't rule out changes to negative gearing
item.description: Jim Chalmers has effectively confirmed Treasury has looked into the impact of limiting one of Australia's most contentious tax breaks for property investors.

item.description: The former US secretary of state and first lady was standing by her husband Bill Clinton at the end of a six-hour deposition.

item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.description: A 20-year-old man has been charged after allegedly planning a terrorist attack on multiple political and religious sites in Perth.

item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.description: T﻿his weekend's strikes by the United States and Israel on Israel come on the back of months of tensions ratcheting up in the Middle East. But the origins go back decades.

item.title: 'Seriously bad advice': British and Irish visitors warned against abusing system by applying for protection vi
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs was forced to issue a public warning against temporary visitors applying for ﻿a $50 protection visa (subclass 866).

item.description: A﻿n expert has said there's no going back for the city, which was until recently seen as a safe, rich, tax-free oasis.

item.description: The government says the 12 offences, mostly domestic and sexual violence, were recommended by the Expert Legal Panel.﻿﻿

item.description: S﻿everal images were posted online by The White House's social media team.

item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader slapped her wrist as her colleagues condemned her comments about Muslims.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap is creeping down, but some major Australian employers are lagging
item.description: Many major Australian brands are failing to turn the dial on the gender pay gap, with some even slipping backwards in the past year, a detailed new report has revealed.

item.description: Under the proposed laws, Victorians who are able to do their job from home will have the right to do so at least two days per week.

item.description: The company has four breweries and about 100 pubs around the globe, including five here in Australia.

item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.description: He hasn't set foot in the country since he was 18, but there are growing calls to install him as leader.

item.description: The byelection will be held on Saturday, May 9, House Speaker Milton Dick announced during Question Time today.

item.description: "I'm not arguing against a public holiday, but we were given four months' notice, so we never budgeted for this in our costings."

item.description: The US Department of Justice has released a series of Epstein files detailing uncorroborated accusations made against Donald Trump had been mistakenly withheld during an earlier review.

item.title: Country by country, here's how the unfolding war is affecting the Middle East and beyond
item.description: The broadening Iran war has ricocheted across the region and beyond, with nearly every country in the Middle East sustaining damage from missile hits, drone strikes or shrapnel.

item.title: Trump says Cuba is soon going to fall: 'I'm going to put Marco over there'
item.description: "I've been watching it for 50 years, and it's fallen right into my lap because of me, it's fallen, but it's nevertheless fallen right into the lap. And we're doing very well."

item.title: Trump rules out talks absent Iran's 'unconditional surrender'
item.description: US President Donald Trump appeared to rule out negotiations with Iran to end the conflict in the Middle East, saying in a social media post there will be no deal absent "unconditional surrender" from Tehran.

item.title: Today in History - March 7: Waitress sued, then kidnapped after getting winning lottery ticket as a tip
item.description: An Alabama waitress was given a winning lottery ticket as a tip on March 7, 1999.

item.title: Indonesia will ban social media for children under 16, communication minister says
item.description: The implementation will start gradually in the south-east Asian nation from March 28.

item.title: Satellite images show the incredible scale of US-Israeli attacks across Iran
item.description: Satellite images have captured the extent of the joint US and Israeli attacks across Iran as all signs point to increased conflict in the region.

item.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian rejected the US President's repeated demands for unconditionally surrender, saying "that's a dream that they should take to their grave".

item.description: Gulf countries say they have intercepted more ballistic missiles and drones as Iran launched another wave of retaliatory strikes.

item.description: A former MP accused of sexually assaulting a teen in a car park toilet has been left in limbo after a jury could not reach a verdict on all charges.

item.description: The Middle East has woken to fresh rounds of air and drone strikes, after Iran named a new supreme leader and the growing conflict took a significant toll on sharemarkets and pushed oil prices to fresh highs.

item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamene, has been named as his successor, Iranian state television has announced.

item.title: Australian mother fighting for life after 'devastating' scooter and truck crash in Bali
item.description: Reese said Alivia was "still in a extremely bad way and not conscious" but was the "strongest" girl she'd ever met.

item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: New footage raises likelihood the US struck an Iranian school where a blast killed at least 165
item.description: New footage shows what an expert investigative group says is likely an American Tomahawk missile hitting a compound in southern Iran, metres from the school where a deadly unclaimed blast killed more than 165 people at the start of the war raging in the Middle East.

item.title: As Trump demands asylum for footballers, other Iranians aren't getting the same treatment in the US
item.description: Even after a bloody crackdown killed thousands of dissidents, the US didn't hesitate to deport those fleeing the regime.

item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.description: O﻿ne of the attackers appears to have been motivated by ISIS.

item.description: T﻿he teacher knew the prank was coming and went outside to catch his students in the act, but was killed, the New York Times reports.

item.title: 'I'm buggered': David Littleproud quits as federal Nationals leader
item.description: A tearful David Littleproud has announced he is resigning as the leader of the federal National Party in a shock development for the junior Coalition party.

item.title: Cops seize water pistol after Sydney TAFE thrown into lockdown
item.description: Police have seized a water pistol and spoken to a 16-year-old girl after a TAFE campus was put in lockdown over suspicious behaviour.

item.title: Iran launches new attacks at Gulf Arab countries as it keeps up pressure on the Middle East
item.description: Incoming missile sirens sounded early in the morning in Dubai in the United Arab Emirates, and in Bahrain, while Saudi Arabia said it had destroyed two drones over its oil-rich eastern region, and Kuwait's National Guard said it had shot down six drones.

item.title: Some independent petrol stations say they have completely run out of fuel as regional shortage worsens
item.description: Transwest Fuel co-owner Sam Clifton said his supply of diesel has now dried up in NSW and Queensland.

item.title: Police car crashes into Sydney family home for second time in two years
item.description: Police say the officers were responding to an assault, and they are assessing the actions of the officers involved.

item.title: Family of boy who security guard died trying to protect pay tribute to Aidan Becker's 'courage and compassion'
item.description: "He showed extraordinary courage and compassion in risking his life to save our son."

item.title: Police hunt three young women after puppy stolen from outside shopping plaza
item.description: "All we want is to have Dora back home with our family".

item.title: Diabetic driver speaks publicly for first time about deadly Daylesford pub crash
item.description: William Swale gave today evidence to Melbourne Coroners Court on the 2023 collision in Daylesford, 110km northwest of Melbourne.

item.title: 'Insult': Teenage killer's appeal upsets slain grandmother Vyleen White's family
item.description: A teenager who killed a grandmother and triggered landmark youth justice laws has appealed his sentence, devastating the victim's family.

item.title: Major superannuation changes to become law, with a push for further tax reform later this year
item.description: The federal government's proposed changes to the way superannuation is taxed will soon become law.

item.title: Iranian football players land in Sydney after five granted humanitarian visas following escape
item.description: More players from the﻿ Iranian women's football team may still seek asylum in Australia after an operation that saw five of their teammates make a break for freedom.

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Police offer $250,000 reward to find accused triple-murderer Julian Ingram
item.description: Police are offering a $250,000 reward for information leading to the arrest of accused murderer Julian Ingram as a small NSW town prepares to farewell his ex-partner, her unborn child and her aunty.

item.title: 'Leave immediately': Hundreds of homes and businesses to be inundated by major flooding in Bundaberg
item.description: The city of ﻿B﻿undaberg on Queensland's central coast is bracing for major flooding similar to the 2010 disaster, with the Burnett River expected to peak at 7.6 metres overnight.

item.title: Today in History - March 11: French superstar dies in freak bathroom accident
item.description: One of France's biggest pop stars died from a freak accident while having a bath on March 11, 1978.

item.title: Australian family left shattered after package containing mother's ashes is cruelly swiped from doorstep
item.description: "Your dealing with someone's family member... and you put instructions allowing them to be dropped on a doorstep?"

item.title: Fears Australia could see another record flu season as 'Super-K' spreads
item.description: T﻿he new and highly infectious 'Super-K' flu is spreading rapidly across the US and Australia.

item.title: World's top oil exporter warns Iran war could have 'catastrophic consequences' for markets
item.description: Saudi Arabia's Aramco, the world's top oil exporter, has warned there could be "catastrophic consequences" for the world's oil markets should the Iran war continue to disrupt access of the Strait of Hormuz – a critical energy-shipping route.

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 over marketing emails
item.description: A﻿CMA found more than 370,000 emails the company sent were in breach of Australian law.

item.title: VPN services exploding in popularity as Pornhub cuts nudity to protest new laws
item.description: The apps allow users to bypass local laws and restrictions and access blocked content.

item.title: Aussies could face $4400 fine over 'dangerous' jerry can act
item.description: The warning comes as some motorists have taken to stockpiling fuel as prices surge across the country.

item.title: Independent petrol stations in regional Queensland running dry, mayor says
item.description: Independent petrol stations in regional Queensland are reportedly starting to run out of fuel as shortages continue to impact rural areas.

item.title: Teachers charged with alleged assault of pupils at school in NSW south-east
item.description: All four educators no longer work at the school and are expected to face court next month.﻿

item.title: Qantas hikes airfares due to Iran war's impact on jet fuel prices
item.description: A﻿s costs to operate flights increase, so has Australian demand.

item.title: Man who mimicked Bondi shooting says actions were 'drunken stupidity'
item.description: Zayne Jason William McMillan, 22, was seen posing and moving his hands as though he was holding a long-barrelled firearm while on a footbridge at Bondi Beach on January 31.

item.title: Police resume Gus Lamont search 165 days after four-year-old disappeared
item.description: South Australia Police today returned to ﻿Gus' family's Oak Park Station homestead.

item.title: 9News Adelaide Riverbend Motorsport
item.description: WIN A MOTORSPORT EXPERIENCE THANKS TO 9NEWS ADELAIDE

item.title: NSW government orders urgent review after foster children found living with triple killer
item.description: Regina Arthurell was able to live in the same house as two foster children aged 12 and 14.

item.title: AS IT HAPPENED: US releases video of missile strikes on minelayers
item.description: US President Donald Trump threatened Iran with "military consequences... at a level never seen before" if i...

item.title: Truth behind 'nuclear trunk' washed up on NSW beach revealed
item.description: P﻿olice were called to the popular tourist spot this morning.

item.title: Two found corrupt over robodebt but Scott Morrison cleared
item.description: A National Anti-Corruption Commission inquiry today found two of six people referred to it for investigation engaged in corrupt conduct, while it cleared the remaining four.

item.title: MP unveils bold tax plan to give every Australian an extra $1643 a year
item.description: The federal government has been urged to slash income tax in a bold proposal that would see 14.7 million working Australians $1643 richer.

item.title: Man to face trial for his wife's murder in Victoria three decades ago
item.description: Craig Freeman, 63, was charged in 2025 with his wife Melinda Freeman's murder, 34 years ago.

item.title: Search under way for missing swimmer on Queensland's Sunshine Coast
item.description: The missing swimmer is described as being in his 60s with a bald head.

item.title: 'It will be breaking point for some families': Three big four banks predict double-dose of interest rate pain
item.description: Market instability fueled by the volatile war in the Middle East pushed two of the big banks to shift their RBA cash rate predictions today.

item.title: Man slashed with a machete after confronting teenagers outside of home
item.description: The Melbourne resident needs to undergo three rounds of surgery.

item.title: Woman charged after stolen puppy located 30 kilometres from home
item.description: A call to Crimestoppers led police to Dora inside a home in Melbourne's south-east.

item.title: Nursing home fined after 45 residents died during COVID
item.description: St Basil's Home for the Aged was sentenced in the Victorian County Court over one of the deadliest nursing home outbreaks in 2020.

item.title: Iranian footballers moved 'immediately' after security scare prompted by change of heart
item.description: O﻿ne of the players called the Iranian embassy, unfortunately disclosing where they were being held.

item.title: Tests paused, students escorted from exam halls after Naplan chaos
item.description: R﻿eports are coming in that the annual test has been struck by technical issues.

item.title: 'Tragic outcome': Married couple from the UK die after being pulled from the water at NSW beach
item.description: Members of the public gave the husband and wife CPR before paramedics arrived, but they could not be revived.

item.title: Sharks reportedly spotted swimming inside netted zone at popular Sydney beach
item.description: The beach and baths at the popular lower north shore spot have been closed as a precaution.

item.title: 'Like a train rumbling past': NSW and ACT shaken by magnitude 4.4 earthquake
item.description: Thousands of people as far afield as Sydney and Batemans Bay felt the shaking from the region's strongest quake in decades.

item.title: Former police officer to face court after alleged drunken crash wiped out parked cars
item.description: ﻿Police will allege the driver, a former NSW police officer, allegedly blew three times over the legal limit.

item.title: Matt Canavan wins vote to become new Nationals leader
item.description: Outspoken Queensland senator Matt Canavan has been elected the new leader of the Nationals following David Littleproud's shock resignation yesterday.

item.title: Melbourne office workers evacuated after suspicious package located
item.description: Police cordoned off the building after reports of a suspicious package just after 10am.

item.title: Two backpackers missing in Queensland as communities grapple with major flooding
item.description: Floodwaters have inundated hundreds of homes and businesses in one city, while in another, the worst is yet to come.

item.title: Evacuation routes blocked amid Perth bushfire emergency
item.description: A raging bushfire in Perth has sparked a fire emergency, as evacuation routes were blocked by the blaze.

item.title: Photos appear to show US Tomahawk missile fragments at site of deadly Iran school strike
item.description: Missile debris that Iranian officials claim was recovered from the deadly strikes which hit an elementary school in southern Iran appears to be from an American Tomahawk cruise missile, according to CNN analysis.

item.title: IEA agrees to record release of emergency oil reserves in an effort to calm surging prices
item.description: The International Energy Agency agreed to release the largest volume of emergency oil reserves in its history.

item.title: Files show British prime minister was warned of 'reputational risk' in appointing Mandelson
item.description: Keir Starmer faces a political storm over the decision to appoint Peter Mandelson as US ambassador after documents released by the British government show officials believed there was a "reputational risk".

item.title: Today in History - March 12: How a stationery store owner crashed an entire economy
item.description: A US stationery store owner began a counterfeiting operation on March 12, 1862 which would crash an entire country's economy.

item.title: Latest health news top stories & analysis
item.description: Latest health news, research and results, scientific discoveries, diets, obesity, cancer, medical procedures and medicine, hospitals, and government health guidelines

item.title: Perth dad fined over 'split-second' seatbelt slip as AI traffic cameras quesioned
item.description: Parents in Western Australia are continuing to speak out against the state's new AI-driven seatbelt cameras, with one Perth dad describing them as an "outrageous overreach".

item.title: Entertainment news headlines - 9News
item.description: Breaking entertainment news including celebrity gossip, film and movie news, music news, award ceremonies, scandals, photos and videos

item.description: Latest technology news stories including consumer electronics, science and space technology, gadgets, internet tech, medical devices, websites and social tech, cryptocurrencies and other technology news headlines

item.description: Latest business news, company tax, government regulations, CEOs, entrepreneurs, customers, and big business strategy

item.title: Politics News - 9News - Latest political news today Australia and World
item.description: Latest political news from Australia and around the world, covering elections, by-elections, our regular leadership spills, poll results, Question Time debates and daily Australian party politics

item.title: World news headlines and international current affairs - 9News
item.description: Latest world news headlines: International breaking news and current affairs from US, Europe, Middle East, Asia, Africa and more. If it happens around the world, you will know about it on 9News

item.title: News Australia - Latest news headlines in Australia
item.description: Today's news in major cities, regional and local areas which can include accident reports, police & emergency responses, criminal and court proceedings or live breaking news

item.title: Ships in Strait of Hormuz caught in conflict crosshairs amid squeeze on oil-rich region
item.description: Iranian attacks against commercial ships, including a Thai cargo ship set ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz, a...
